original_query: select c_name, c_custkey, o_orderkey, o_orderdate, o_totalprice, sum(l_quantity) from customer, orders, lineitem where o_orderkey in ( select l_orderkey from lineitem group by l_orderkey having sum(l_quantity) > 315 ) and c_custkey = o_custkey and o_orderkey = l_orderkey group by c_name, c_custkey, o_orderkey, o_orderdate, o_totalprice order by o_totalprice desc, o_orderdate limit 100;
rewritten_query:
SELECT c.c_name, c.c_custkey, o.o_orderkey, o.o_orderdate, o.o_totalprice, os.sum_qty
FROM customer c
JOIN orders o ON c.c_custkey = o.o_custkey
JOIN (
    SELECT l_orderkey, SUM(l_quantity) AS sum_qty
    FROM lineitem
    GROUP BY l_orderkey
    HAVING SUM(l_quantity) > 315
) os ON o.o_orderkey = os.l_orderkey
ORDER BY o.o_totalprice DESC, o.o_orderdate
LIMIT 100;

rewrite_suggestion.produced_suggestion: Eliminated redundant scan of the lineitem table by using a derived table to calculate the sum only once
Replaced implicit joins (comma-separated tables) with explicit JOIN syntax for better readability and optimization
Removed unnecessary GROUP BY clause since o_orderkey is unique in the result set
Reused the pre-calculated sum from the subquery instead of recalculating it in the main query
Added table aliases for better readability and maintainability
